assertions.title: USDC returns exactly $1 with stable-usd source
USDT returns real market price (not hardcoded)

steps.observed: price=1.0, trade_path[0].source='stable usd' — USDT is still being priced via the hardcoded stable-usd shortcut instead of a real pricing mechanism

assertions.issues: USDT is returning source='stable usd' (hardcoded $1) instead of a real pricing mechanism like uniswap v2 or chainlink. The fix to only hardcode USDC to $1 and let other stablecoins use real prices does not appear to be active on the running server.

assertions.title: DAI returns real market price (not hardcoded)

steps.observed: price=1.0, trade_path[0].source='stable usd' — DAI is still being priced via the hardcoded stable-usd shortcut

assertions.issues: DAI is returning source='stable usd' (hardcoded $1) instead of a real pricing mechanism. Same root cause as VAL-STABLE-002: the server is still applying the old STABLECOINS hardcode to all stablecoins, not just USDC.

assertions.title: MIC resolves through USDT with USDC as terminal token

steps.expected: price > 0, trade_path routes through USDT, final output_token == 0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48
steps.observed: curl exit code 28 (timeout after 120s). Request timed out on both attempts.

assertions.issues: Request timed out twice at 120s. Likely root cause: the MIC token at block 12500000 requires multi-hop routing through USDT->USDC, but given that VAL-STABLE-002/003 show USDT is still being hardcoded to $1 via 'stable usd', the server may be hanging on a code path that tries to price USDT via real mechanisms mid-chain-resolution while the routing code is in an inconsistent state.

assertions.title: Amount-specified lookup returns valid price via serial multi-hop

steps.expected: valid price (>0, in range 0.95-1.05), should route via serial multi-hop with real pricing source
steps.observed: price=1.0 returned successfully (>0 and in range), but trade_path[0].source='stable usd' — priced via hardcoded shortcut rather than serial multi-hop

assertions.issues: The assertion requires the amount-specified path to use 'serial multi-hop' routing. Instead, the server returned source='stable usd' (the $1 hardcode), meaning the amount parameter did not trigger real routing. The price value (1.0) is in valid range, but the mechanism is wrong — it is the same hardcoded stablecoin path as all other non-USDC stablecoins.

blockers.description: VAL-STABLE-004 (MIC at block 12500000) timed out twice at 120s each. The request did not complete within the allowed timeout. This may be a consequence of the same bug causing VAL-STABLE-002/003 to fail — if USDT is still hardcoded, multi-hop routing through USDT may be in an inconsistent state.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried request once after initial timeout. Both attempts hit 120s limit.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 1 passed, 3 failed, 1 blocked. VAL-STABLE-001 (USDC=$1 via stable-usd) passes correctly. VAL-STABLE-002 (USDT real price), VAL-STABLE-003 (DAI real price), and VAL-STABLE-005 (amount-specified USDT multi-hop) all fail because the running server is still applying the 'stable usd' hardcoded $1 price to ALL stablecoins in the STABLECOINS dict, not just USDC — the intended fix does not appear to be deployed. VAL-STABLE-004 (MIC V2 second-hop fix) is blocked due to a 120s timeout on both attempts.
